trade.summary: Negligible direct bilateral trade due to deep political hostility. Limited informal trade through intermediaries. Both compete as major oil exporters and are rivals within OPEC+.

diplomatic.summary: Cina-brokered diplomatic normalisation in March 2023 restored ambassadors, but underlying tensions remain. Sectarian (Shia-Sunni) rivalry, proxy conflicts, and regional hegemony competition persist.
recent_incidents: Houthi attacks on Saudi infrastructure backed by Iran
Proxy competition in Iraq, Syria, Lebanon, and Yemen

military.summary: No direct military conflict but intense proxy warfare. Iran backs Houthis (Yemen), Hezbollah, and Shia militias in Iraq. Arabia Saudita leads coalition against Houthis.
incidents: Houthi drone and missile attacks on Saudi Aramco facilities

financial.summary: No formal financial ties. Iran under extensive sanctions blocking normal financial flows. Arabia Saudita central to Western-aligned petrodollar system.

energy.summary: Both are top-tier oil producers and OPEC+ members. Competition over market share and pricing is a persistent source of tension.

scientific.summary: Minimal scientific exchange. Religious and political rivalry extends to academic and cultural spheres. Some limited contact through OIC forums.
